docs.title: CMSAPI
docs.text:
Python-based REST API for retrieving, analyzing and consolidating content published in UniBE CMS and other information systems.

OpenAPI Docs

  • v3 generated by Swagger / ReDoc
  • v2 generated by Swagger (deprecated)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The implementation of a RESTful API for UniBE CMS relies on the FastAPI Library. This should enable a more lightweight Python development mode alongside the historically grown Zope stack1.

Additionally, CMSAPI makes use of SQLModel to implement the object relational mapping, Typer for the CLI admin tool, and is served by Uvicorn in testing and production deployments.

This presentation (see pdf) gives an overview of the background and objectives:

docs.title: Objectives
docs.text:
The CMSAPI serves to decouple the established and solid backend content management system ZMS from frontend web development (Single-page apps, Progressive web apps, Mobile apps native, hybrid, etc.), which is nowadays extremely volatile compared to the backend.

JavaScript-based frontend technologies are a highly dynamic environment with exciting hype cycles of constant rise and fall — naming Angular, React, Vue or Svelte.

The same applies to native app development with Swift, Objective-C, Java, Kotlin for the mobile operating systems iOS and Android or SDKs such as Flutter using Dart programming language for the development of cross-platform apps.

Decouple frontend from backend development

Basically, the over decades proven mechanisms of the backend CMS Template Engine2 should be "preserved" from "overwhelming" frontend logic.

On the other hand, the possibilities of current frontend JavaScript Frameworks for content presentation should be opened up, e.g. for Scrollytelling3.

docs.title: Implementation
docs.text:
To achieve decoupling, the development of a headless mode for ZMS was started (internal branch zms-headless)4.

This allows direct access to content objects stored in ZODB without the need to run a Zope Application server in context of CMSAPI — the corresponding Zope Python packages are needed only for database connection and data deserialization.

This architecture provides a decoupled work on the interface, so the CMSAPI can be developed and deployed independently of running ZMS backend system(s). Thus, additional Python libraries and language features can be used easily and flexibly – no matter if they are present or not in the existing ZMS backend system.

See the Examples and the interactive OpenAPI docs.

Info

Up to v2, the CMSAPI is based on the Flask framework with (confusing) extension modules for REST implementation as well as ZODB connectivity.

The v3 is a new implementation based on FastAPI, which also includes an object relation mapping of ZMSModels. Using the command line tool cmsapi.admin, data from the ZODB can be stored in a PostgreSQL database for high performance querying (including offset/limit for pagination, order/group by, joins, etc.) and can be combined with data from other data sources (e.g. to aggregate News/Events from other systems).

docs.title: Next steps / ideas
docs.text:
The whole approach can also be used to move specific logic from the backend to the interface

  • for data searching and filtering using SQL or GraphQL queries,
  • for connections to third-party service providers like OpenSearch engine,
  • for asynchronous and distributed processing with Message brokers — see also AsyncAPI for such event-driven architectures.

Responses of data queries and/or transformations could be re-integrated back into the ZMI — the user interface of the ZMS backend can be seen as a specific frontend web app that receives and sends messages from/to the CMSAPI using WebSockets.

Further reading

Guides/TutorialsConcepts/ReferencesContexts/Backgrounds
  • FastAPI WebSockets
  • FastAPI Background Tasks
  • FastAPI Security
  • FastAPI Mount Sub Applications
  • FastAPI Links and Articles
  • Contentful API basics
  • Contentful CMS as Code
  • Content Management API
  • Content Delivery API
  • Content Sync API
  • Nordic APIs Security blog
  • How to Secure API Endpoints
  • What Is OAuth? A Breakdown for Beginners
  • OAuth Flows and Powers
  • Deep Dive into OAuth and OpenID Connect

Further development

Currently, the focus of CMSAPI is exclusively on retrieving public data and content from the ZODB.

Write access to the ZODB would still need to be evaluated if required — as would the consideration of valid permissions to access the database (ACLs).

Further relevant topics include other security concerns (OAuth, WAF) and how to control the delivery using caching, rate limits, etc.

  1. see Zope Overview and Timeline ↩

  2. see Zope Page Templates (ZPT) using Template Attribute Language (TAL) ↩

  3. see Pull request and Discussion for a Scrollytelling frontend web app based on Fetch API to retrieve content from ZMS backend ↩

  4. see GitHub Enterprise Server with interal repository at branch zms-headless (UniBE VPN and Campus Account needed) ↩

docs.title: CMSAPI Examples
docs.text:
Python-based REST API for retrieving, analyzing and consolidating content published in UniBE CMS and other information systems.

OpenAPI Docs

  • v3 generated by Swagger / ReDoc
  • v2 generated by Swagger (deprecated)

docs.title: Data retrival
docs.text:
The endpoints which are backed by a PostgreSQL database (object relation mapping) support the query parameters offset and limit for pagination.

To filter the response values, e.g. date_after or sections can be used.

Furthermore lang is used for language specific queries.

Example

PaginationFilterAggregation 
curl -X 'GET' \
  'https://api.cms.test.unibe.ch/v3/news?lang=de&offset=0&limit=20' \
  -H 'accept: application/json'

{
  "offset": 0,
  "limit": 20,
  "total": 243,
  "data": [
    {
      "title": "Wie geht (mehr) Reproduzierbarkeit?",
      "date": "2023-01-05T12:03:01+01:00",
      "url": "http://www.uniaktuell.unibe.ch/2022/wie_geht_mehr_reproduzierbarkeit/index_ger.html",
      "infos": "\n\nSwiss Reproducibility Network",

    ...

  -H 'accept: application/json'

docs.title: Content retrival
docs.text:
The ZMS Content endpoint provides instant retrival of content stored in ZODB by path or uuid query parameters.

It utilizes the ZMS default XML export function xml = zodb.toXml(deep=True|False). The boolean parameter deep controls the processing of children in the content tree.

The default XML response is automatically converted into JSON using xmltodict Python library xmltodict.parse(xml).

Caveat

The current implementation is a simple solution to proof the concept.

These response schemes need to be revised and adapted based on concrete use cases — especially for better client-side processing of multilingual content.

Example

XMLJSON 
curl -X 'GET' \
  'https://api.cms.test.unibe.ch/v3/zms/content?path=/unibe/uniapp/content&deep=false&data=xml' \
  -H 'accept: application/xml'

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
  <ZMS uid="uid:9cc058e9-7108-41ea-bce5-f88a0e299988" id_fix="content">
    <attr_dc_coverage>global.ger</attr_dc_coverage>
    <created_uid>cmeier</created_uid>
    <created_dt><![CDATA[time.struct_time(tm_year=2020, tm_mon=3, tm_mday=9, tm_hour=13, tm_min=26, tm_sec=33, tm_wday=0, tm_yday=69, tm_isdst=0)]]></created_dt>
    <change_uid>
      <lang id="ger">lm19s458</lang>
      <lang id="fra">lm19s458</lang>
      <lang id="eng">lm19s458</lang>
    </change_uid>
    <change_dt>
      <lang id="ger"><![CDATA[time.struct_time(tm_year=2021, tm_mon=4, tm_mday=22, tm_hour=9, tm_min=50, tm_sec=15, tm_wday=3, tm_yday=112, tm_isdst=1)]]></lang>
      <lang id="fra"><![CDATA[time.struct_time(tm_year=2021, tm_mon=3, tm_mday=18, tm_hour=11, tm_min=33, tm_sec=13, tm_wday=3, tm_yday=77, tm_isdst=0)]]></lang>
      <lang id="eng"><![CDATA[time.struct_time(tm_year=2021, tm_mon=3, tm_mday=18, tm_hour=11, tm_min=33, tm_sec=0, tm_wday=3, tm_yday=77, tm_isdst=0)]]></lang>
    </change_dt>
    <active>
      <lang id="ger">1</lang>
      <lang id="fra">1</lang>
      <lang id="eng">1</lang>
    </active>
    <titlealt>
      <lang id="ger"><![CDATA[UniBE Mobile]]></lang>
      <lang id="fra"><![CDATA[UniBE Mobile]]></lang>
      <lang id="eng"><![CDATA[UniBE Mobile]]></lang>
    </titlealt>

  </ZMS>

  'https://api.cms.test.unibe.ch/v3/zms/content?path=/unibe/uniapp/content&deep=false&data=json' \
  -H 'accept: application/json'

docs.title: Alternative formats
docs.text:
The following examples provide use cases integrating two handy Python libraries:

  • anytree to build tree data structures and
  • ics for iCalendar support.

Example

TreeiCalendar 
curl -X 'GET' \
  'https://api.cms.test.unibe.ch/v3/sections?lang=de&tree=true' \
  -H 'accept: application/json'

{
  "title": "UniBE",
  "children": [
    {
      "domain": "www.unibe.ch",
      "title": "Portal",
      "type": "Home",
      "path": "/unibe/portal/content",
      "uuid": "9c92af4f-6e95-4391-86d5-76eb8ad48360",
      "children": [
        {
          "domain": "www.medizin.unibe.ch",
          "title": "Medizinische Fakultät",
          "type": "Fakultaet",
          "path": "/unibe/portal/fak_medizin/content",
          "uuid": "6d35e100-4ff0-47fa-ae61-a2793dd4f949",
          "children": [
            {
              "domain": "",
              "title": "Bereich Dienstleistungsbetriebe",
              "type": "Departement",
              "path": "/unibe/portal/fak_medizin/ber_dlb/content",
              "uuid": "fc6b0cc3-33f9-4d98-b7c4-8119596c8a09",
              "children": [
                {
                  "domain": "",
                  "title": "Forensisch-Psychiatrischer Dienst",
                  "type": "Institut",
                  "path": "/unibe/portal/fak_medizin/ber_dlb/dienst_fpd/content",
                  "uuid": "36a60ff5-faa1-42e0-bf25-497e1b83f525"
                },
    ...

https://api.cms.test.unibe.ch/v3/events/calender.ics

docs.title: Cluster deployment
docs.text:
The CMSAPI stack consists mainly of two services:

  • a few API containers which provide the endpoints
  • a single ADM container which processes the object relation mapping based on cron jobs during office hours by /app/bin/python -m cmsapi.admin.main update --metaobj all >> /app/log/api-cron.log

The database connection strings are set in the environment variables ZODB_STORAGE and SQLDB_STORAGE.

Example

API containersADM container 
  api-v3:
    image: ep-devops.id.unibe.ch:5000/id/unibe-cmsapi-v3:rc-3.0.0
    volumes:
      - logs:/app/log
    environment:
      - PYTHONUNBUFFERED=true
      - ZODB_STORAGE=zeo://idcmsdata3.unibe.ch:7001  # at www.cmstest.unibe.ch (synced daily with PROD data)
      - SQLDB_STORAGE=postgresql://pgtest.unibe.ch:5432/unibe_cmsapi?sslmode=require&application_name=CMSAPI
      - FORWARDED_ALLOW_IPS=*  # trust proxy headers of Kemp => https https://www.uvicorn.org/deployment/
    networks:
      - worker
      - cms_network
    secrets:
      - source: test-unibe-cmsapi-v3-sqldb-credentials
        target: sqldb.credentials
    deploy:
      replicas: 3
      restart_policy:
        condition: on-failure
      resources:
        limits:
          cpus: "1.0"
          memory: 768M
      labels:
        - "traefik.enable=true"
        - "traefik.docker.lbswarm=true"
        - "traefik.docker.network=worker"
        - "traefik.http.services.test-unibe-cmsapi.loadbalancer.server.port=5003"
        - "traefik.http.services.test-unibe-cmsapi.loadbalancer.passhostheader=true"
        - "traefik.http.routers.test-unibe-cmsapi.rule=(Host(\"api.cms.test.unibe.ch\") && PathPrefix(\"/v3\")"
        - "traefik.http.routers.test-unibe-cmsapi.entrypoints=websecure"
        - "traefik.http.routers.test-unibe-cmsapi.tls=true"
    ports:
      - 5003

  adm-v3:
    image: ep-devops.id.unibe.ch:5000/id/unibe-cmsapi-v3:rc-3.0.0
    volumes:
      - logs:/app/log
    environment:
      - PYTHONUNBUFFERED=true
      - ZODB_STORAGE=zeo://idcmsdata3.unibe.ch:7001  # at www.cmstest.unibe.ch (synced daily with PROD data)
      - SQLDB_STORAGE=postgresql://pgtest.unibe.ch:5432/unibe_cmsapi?sslmode=require&application_name=CMSAPI
      - RUN_CRONJOBS=true  # init_scripts startUp in this dedicated admin container only
    networks:
      - worker
      - cms_network
    secrets:
      - source: test-unibe-cmsapi-v3-sqldb-credentials
        target: sqldb.credentials
    deploy:
      replicas: 1
      restart_policy:
        condition: on-failure
      resources:
        limits:
          cpus: "1.0"
          memory: 768M

docs.title: Status codes
docs.text:
• 200 OK Successful request
  • 404 Not Found No such object
  • 500 Internal Server Error Something went wrong
  • 503 Service Unavailable Service is down (maybe for maintenance)

docs.title: Localization
docs.text:
Localization is defined in each request with query param or URL param. Accepted values are:

  • de - Deutsch
  • en - English
  • fr - Français

The fallback language handling should be implemented in the CMS API.

Date and datetime do not rely on localization and are always returned in standard format.

docs.title: Query Params
docs.text: All GET endpoints which return a list support offset based pagination, startAt/endAt and uuids filters.

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Parameter Description limit int optional Maximal number of elements per query. Min: 1. offset int optional Current offset. Default is 0 which is the first element. Must be >= 0. startAt string optional The date start filter. e.g. "2022-10-06" endAt string optional The date end filter. e.g. "2022-10-30" uuids* string[] The uuids of the newscontainers. e.g. ["fb3f47eb-f2d3-4889-ae48-c72f24b7900c", "581e2cf8-fe7f-4e72-898d-92512d9596f0"]

docs.title: Example Request
docs.text: $ curl https://announcement-api.unibe.ch/events?limit=10&offset=2&startAt=2022-10-06&endAt=2022-10-30&uuids=fb3f47eb-f2d3-4889-ae48-c72f24b7900c&uuids=581e2cf8-fe7f-4e72-898d-92512d9596f0

docs.title: Example Response
docs.text:
{
  "offset": 5,
  "limit": 10,
  "total": 20,
  "data": [
    //...
  ]
}

docs.title: TIMESTAMPS
docs.text: All timestamp are returned in ISO8601 format in UTC with fields ending in postfix At. Example: "publishedAt": "2015-07-01T00:55:47Z"

docs.title: Category resource
docs.text: Field Description id string Category ID title string Category title link string Link to category on unibe.ch site subCategories [Category], optional Sub categories of category

docs.title: HTTP REQUEST
docs.text: GET /v2/announcements/categories/news

docs.title: Example response (200)
docs.text:
{
  "id": "581e2cf8-fe7f-4e72-898d-92512d9596f0",
  "title": "Portal",
  "link": "https://www.unibe.ch/unibe/portal/content/e681",
  "subCategories": [
    {
      "id": "cba8131b-1c7d-4992-82c5-ddfb283fd854",
      "title": "Theologische Fakultät",

docs.title: News resource
docs.text: Field Description titlestring  News title imageUrl string, optional News cover image publishedAt timestamp Publish date link string Link to news item categoryLink string Link to the category of news item content string Content of news item

docs.title: EventExtraInfo resource
docs.text: Field Description buildingstring, optional Building where event may happen. location string, optional Address of event room string, optional Room of event speakers string, optional Speakers of event

docs.title: Event resource
docs.text: Field Description titlestring Event title imageUrl string, optional Event cover image startAt timestamp Start date endAt timestamp End date extraInfo EventExtraInfo More information about the event link string Link to event categoryLink string Link to the category of event topic string, optional Topic of event item

docs.title: HTTP REQUEST
docs.text: GET /v2/announcements/news

docs.title: ARGUMENTS
docs.text:
Parameter Type Required Description categories [string] Required Array of category ids (UUIDs) publishedBeforAt timestamp  Required Show news published before this timestamp

Arguments for pagination apply too!

docs.title: Example response (200)
docs.text:
{
  "offset": 5,
  "limit": 10,
  "total": 20,
  "data": [
    {
      "title": "Wartungsarbeiten der Webseiten der UB Bern",
      "image_url": "https://www.unibe.ch/unibe/portal/content/e681/e1087345/ub_alert1_ger.jpg",
      "published_at": "2021-06-09T15:34:42+02:00",
      "link": "https://www.unibe.ch/universitaet/dienstleistungen/universitaetsbibliothek/index_ger.html",
      "category_link": "https://www.unibe.ch/unibe/portal/content/e681",
      "content": "Wegen Wartungsarbeiten am Dienstag, 15.06.2021 von 7:00 bis 9:00 können die Webseiten der Universitätsbibliothek Bern in diesem Zeitfenster kurzzeitig nicht erreichbar sein."
    }
    //...
  ]
}

docs.title: HTTP REQUEST
docs.text: GET /v2/announcements/events

docs.title: ARGUMENTS
docs.text:
Parameter Type Required Description categories [string] Required Array of category ids (UUIDs) startAfterAt timestamp  Required Show events after this timestamp

Arguments for pagination apply too!

docs.title: Example response (200)
docs.text:
{
  "offset": 5,
  "limit": 10,
  "total": 20,
  "data": [
    {
      "title": "MIC Research Day 2021",
      "image_url": "https://www.unibe.ch/unibe/portal/content/e681/e1087345/ub_alert1_ger.jpg",
      "start_at": "2021-07-07T12:00",
      "end_at": "2021-07-07T18:00",
      "extra_info": {
        "building": "UniS, Schanzeneckstrasse 1",
        "location": "3012 Bern",
        "room": "Vorlesungsraum S003"
      },
      "link": "https://www.mic.unibe.ch…ts/mic_research_day_2021",
      "category_link": "https://agenda.unibe.ch",
      "topic": "Event – Microscopy Imaging Center"
    }
    //...
  ]
}
